limiting score variance and statistical sensitivity,
particularly in females. When age is partialled out
(CVR~mimic~), the indirect pathway
(CVR → HVR → Cognition) disappears — the a-path is
null, suggesting the FRS-hippocampal association in
this sample is primarily carried by the age component
of FRS rather than by cardiovascular risk factors
per se. This does not invalidate FRS as a clinical
tool — age is itself a potent marker of cumulative
vascular exposure
[@villeneuve_impact_2009; @dagostino_general_2008] —
but it does mean that FRS-brain associations in
elderly cohorts cannot be straightforwardly
attributed to modifiable cardiovascular factors.

The negative total cholesterol loading (see
@tbl-mimic-loadings) likely reflects widespread
statin use in this elderly clinical sample: treated
individuals have lower cholesterol despite elevated
underlying cardiovascular risk, inverting the
expected direction of association with the latent
CVR factor. Common-sample standardization (z-scoring
both FRS and CVR~mimic~ on the same N with complete
data for both measures) ensures regression
coefficients express effects per 1 SD change on an
identical scale — without this step, the a-path
dissociation could reflect scaling artifacts rather
than substantive predictor properties. Prior studies
reporting FRS-brain relationships in elderly samples
[@zsoldos_association_2020; @seshadri_stroke_2004; @song_associations_2020; @meusel_cardiovascular_2019]
may therefore reflect a mixture of age-driven and
cardiovascular effects that is difficult to
disentangle without age-adjustment.

## Dissociating Moderation from Mediation

As outlined in the analytical roadmap, the two
modelling frameworks test complementary questions.
The LME 3-way interaction
(YRS × CVR × HVR → Cognition) tests moderation:
does CVR modify how the HVR-cognition relationship
evolves over time? The LGCM a-path
(CVR → HVR slope; @fig-mediation) tests mediation:
does CVR affect cognition through hippocampal
decline?

A different pattern emerged for CVR~mimic~: the
LGCM a-path was null across all six models,
suggesting age-adjusted CVR does not accelerate
hippocampal atrophy. Yet the LME 3-way interaction
was significant in
`r n_of_total.fn(abs_lme$cvr_f_sig, 3L)` female
domains
`r if(nchar(abs_lme$cvr_f_sig_doms) > 0) sprintf("(%s)", abs_lme$cvr_f_sig_doms) else ""`
only, with
`r n_of_total.fn(abs_lme$cvr_m_sig, 3L)` significant
in males. This dissociation represents moderation
without mediation through hippocampal volume:
CVR~mimic~ does not accelerate hippocampal atrophy
(a-path null), yet it may modify how hippocampal
integrity relates to cognitive performance over
time. This pattern is consistent with a cognitive
reserve framework
[@stern_cognitive_2012; @rabin_interactive_2018; @iadecola_framingham_2020]:
cardiovascular risk may reduce the brain's capacity
to maintain cognitive function at a given level of
structural integrity, without directly driving
structural decline. This interpretation is further
supported by the significant CVR~mimic~ direct
effect (c') for Female Memory (@fig-mediation),
which indicates CVR~mimic~ can influence cognitive
decline through a non-hippocampal pathway even when
the a-path is null. However, the CVR~mimic~ LME
evidence rests on a single domain in one sex, and
this isolated finding should be interpreted
cautiously until replicated.

The HVR-cognition coupling (b-path) was consistent
across CVR predictors, in line with prior LGCM
coupling studies
[@muniz-terrera_latent_2017; @bollen_latent_2006].

## Sex Differences

CVR~mimic~ LME effects reached significance in
`r n_of_total.fn(abs_lme$cvr_m_sig, 3L)` male
domains and
`r n_of_total.fn(abs_lme$cvr_f_sig, 3L)` female
domains
`r if(nchar(abs_lme$cvr_f_sig_doms) > 0) sprintf("(%s)", abs_lme$cvr_f_sig_doms) else ""`.
The female CVR~mimic~ Executive finding is notable as
the only CVR~mimic~ LME effect observed in either sex
and warrants targeted investigation in independent
samples. Female FRS variance compression limits
statistical sensitivity — the FRS findings in females
may be primarily driven by age-weighting. Sex
differences in cardiovascular risk profiles
[@ferretti_sex_2018; @mielke_consideration_2022],
differential APOE ε4 effects, and hormonal
influences on vascular regulation may all contribute
to the observed sex asymmetry in CVR-brain
relationships. Covariates (age, education, APOE ε4)
exhibited expected effects across all models
(Appendix B, @tbl-lme-covariates and
@tbl-cvr-lme-covariates).

## HVR-Cognitive Coupling

The HVR slope → Cognitive slope relationship
(b-path) was consistent regardless of whether FRS or
CVR~mimic~ was the predictor (@fig-mediation).
The b-path was significant in
`r n_of_total.fn(abs_stats$frs_b_sig, 6L)` FRS and
`r n_of_total.fn(abs_stats$cvr_b_sig, 6L)`
CVR~mimic~ models, supporting HVR decline as
a reliable marker of cognitive decline
[@kramer_longitudinal_2007; @oschwald_brain_2020]
and extending earlier evidence that HVR captures
hippocampal integrity more sensitively than raw
volume [@schoemaker_hippocampal-ventricle_2019;
@fernandez-lozano_enhanced_2025;
@bruno_hippocampal_2016].

Parallel process coupling showed a complementary
pattern: significant in all three male models but
only Executive Function in females (one of three).
This sex asymmetry likely reflects lower statistical
power in the smaller female subsample rather than a
biological absence of coupling, given that mediation
b-paths (which condition on a predictor) were
significant in
`r n_of_total.fn(abs_stats$frs_b_sig, 6L)` models
for both sexes. Language mediation b-paths were
non-significant for both sexes, though Language
parallel process coupling reached significance in
males (consistent with @sec-slope-coupling) but not
females — a pattern compatible with language being
a later-declining domain with less variance to
capture at this disease stage. The
hippocampal-cognitive coupling is not an artifact of
CVR measurement — it persists across predictor
specifications and analysis frameworks.

## Methodological Considerations

The fixed-quadratic LGCM captures population-level
curvature but constrains individual quadratic
variation, so slope estimates primarily reflect
average rates of change. This makes the a-path null
finding conservative — if CVR~mimic~ had
individual-level non-linear effects on hippocampal
trajectories, these would require a fully random
quadratic model to detect, further strengthening the
conclusion that the FRS a-path reflects
age-weighting.

EDT linearity tests confirmed significant non-linear
trajectories across all models (Appendix B,
@tbl-linearity), motivating the fixed-quadratic
specification [@newsom_longitudinal_2015]. Fully
random quadratic models failed to converge due to
near-zero quadratic variance. EDT as the time metric
accounts for heterogeneous disease progression,
though it introduces progmod dependence.

Sensitivity analyses (Appendix C) showed HC z-score
effects were weaker than HVR effects
(@tbl-hc-comparison), attrition patterns suggest
observed effects may underestimate true relationships
(@tbl-attrition-baseline), and raw-volume results
were concordant with z-scored analyses.

## Clinical Implications

In clinical trials targeting cardiovascular risk
reduction for dementia prevention, FRS remains a
valid summary of overall vascular burden — including
the age-driven component that itself reflects
cumulative exposure
[@villeneuve_impact_2009; @dagostino_general_2008].
However, when the research question concerns
cardiovascular effects independent of aging,
age-adjusted CVR measures such as CVR~mimic~ provide
a necessary complement
[@ko_calibration_2020; @zhang_bayesian_2024]. HVR
provides a more sensitive neuroimaging endpoint than
hippocampal volume alone, and its consistent
cognitive coupling supports use as a surrogate
outcome in intervention studies. The isolated
CVR~mimic~ Executive Function finding (one of six tests,
non-significant after FDR) requires replication
before clinical interpretation.

## Limitations

This study used a single cohort (ADNI), and
replication in other aging samples is needed.
UKB-trained GAMLSS normative models showed a
systematic negative z-score offset in the ADNI
comparable subsample due to age distribution
mismatch; within-ADNI relative comparisons remain
valid, but absolute z-scores should be interpreted
cautiously across cohorts. CVR~mimic~ indicators
were limited to available CVRF data; additional
indicators could improve the latent CVR measure.
CVR was measured cross-sectionally, and the
amyloid-positive sample limits generalizability to
individuals without AD pathology.

Our coefficient comparison tests share the same
resampling framework as primary mediation inference,
with no independent validation.
Individually-varying time scores preclude CFI, TLI,
and RMSEA computation — model adequacy was assessed
via information criteria and nested LRTs only. The
MIMIC model used only 5 indicators; richer models
may yield different CVR~mimic~ values. Bootstrap
mediation CIs (N = `r med_n.lst$Male` males,
`r med_n.lst$Female` females) may be liberal with
these modest sample sizes.

## Conclusions

How CVR is measured determines whether it appears
to influence hippocampal-cognitive trajectories: FRS
produces significant indirect effects through
hippocampal decline, but age-adjusted CVR~mimic~
does not. Because age is both a legitimate marker of
cumulative vascular exposure and an independent
driver of neurodegeneration
[@villeneuve_impact_2009], the most parsimonious
explanation is that FRS-hippocampal associations in
this elderly sample are primarily carried by the age
component of the score. Suggestive single-domain evidence
(Female Executive LME 3-way) hints that age-adjusted
CVR may still modulate the brain-cognition
relationship through reserve-like mechanisms. The
hippocampal-cognitive coupling persists regardless of
CVR measurement, supporting HVR as a reliable
biomarker target.

Future directions include replication in independent
aging cohorts (e.g., WRAP, AIBL), longitudinal CVR
measurement to capture within-person CVR
trajectories, and intervention studies testing
whether CVR reduction preserves the HVR-cognition
coupling. Beyond cardiovascular risk, the
MIMIC-based age-adjustment framework presented here
applies to any composite score whose constituent
parts include a variable that independently predicts
the outcome.

---

# Ethics Approval Statement {.unnumbered}

Data used in preparation of this article were
obtained from the ADNI database. ADNI was approved by
the Institutional Review Boards of all participating
institutions. Written informed consent was obtained
from all participants.

# Author Contributions {.unnumbered}

S.F-L. conceived the study, developed the analysis
pipeline, performed all analyses, and wrote the
manuscript. S.V. provided critical review of the
study design and interpretation. D.L.C. supervised
the project, provided critical review, and approved
the final manuscript.

# Acknowledgments {.unnumbered}

Data collection and sharing for this project was
funded by the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging
Initiative (ADNI) (National Institutes of Health
Grant U01 AG024904) and DOD ADNI (Department of
Defense award number W81XWH-12-2-0012). ADNI is
funded by the National Institute on Aging, the
National Institute of Biomedical Imaging and
Bioengineering, and through generous contributions
from the following: Alzheimer's Association;
Alzheimer's Drug Discovery Foundation; Araclon
Biotech; BioClinica, Inc.; Biogen Idec Inc.;
Bristol-Myers Squibb Company; Eisai Inc.; Elan
Pharmaceuticals, Inc.; Eli Lilly and Company;
EuroImmun; F. Hoffmann-La Roche Ltd and its
affiliated company Genentech, Inc.; Fujirebio; GE
Healthcare; IXICO Ltd.; Janssen Alzheimer
Immunotherapy Research & Development, LLC.; Johnson &
Johnson Pharmaceutical Research & Development LLC.;
Medpace, Inc.; Merck & Co., Inc.; Meso Scale
Diagnostics, LLC.; NeuroRx Research; Neurotrack
Technologies; Novartis Pharmaceuticals Corporation;
Pfizer Inc.; Piramal Imaging; Servier; Synarc Inc.;
and Takeda Pharmaceutical Company. The Canadian
Institutes of Health Research is providing funds to
support ADNI clinical sites in Canada. Private sector
contributions are facilitated by the Foundation for
the National Institutes of Health (www.fnih.org). The
grantee organization is the Northern California
Institute for Research and Education, and the study
is coordinated by the Alzheimer's Disease Cooperative
Study at the University of California, San Diego.
ADNI data are disseminated by the Laboratory for
Neuro Imaging at the University of Southern
California.

*Data used in preparation of this article were
obtained from the ADNI database
(adni.loni.usc.edu). As such, the investigators
within the ADNI contributed to the design and
implementation of ADNI and/or provided data but did
not participate in analysis or writing of this
report. A complete listing of ADNI investigators can
be found at:
http://adni.loni.usc.edu/wp-content/uploads/how_to_apply/ADNI_Acknowledgement_List.pdf*

# Conflict of Interest Statement {.unnumbered}

The authors declare no conflicts of interest.

# Data Availability Statement {.unnumbered}

This research was conducted using ADNI data
(adni.loni.usc.edu). Data are available to qualified
researchers through the ADNI data sharing platform.
Analysis code is available at
https://github.com/soffiafdz/hvr-cvr-mediation.

\newpage

# Supplementary Material {.unnumbered}

Appendices A--C are provided as supplementary
material and contain additional tables, figures, and
analyses referenced in the main text.

:::{=latex}
\appendix
\setcounter{table}{0}
\setcounter{figure}{0}
\renewcommand{\thetable}{A\arabic{table}}
\renewcommand{\thefigure}{A\arabic{figure}}
:::

# Normative Transfer Validation


For transfer validation, we identified a comparable
ADNI subsample of `r adni_demo$n_total`
amyloid-negative cognitively unimpaired participants
(`r sprintf("%.0f%%", adni_demo$pct_female)` female)
within the UKB age range. Despite matching on amyloid
status and cognitive normality, the ADNI comparable
subsample was approximately
`r sprintf("%.0f", adni_demo$age_mean - ukb_demo$age_mean)`
years older on average (mean
`r sprintf("%.1f", adni_demo$age_mean)` vs
`r sprintf("%.1f", ukb_demo$age_mean)` years;
@fig-normative-validation A), concentrating in the
upper tail of the UKB age distribution. HVR z-scores
showed a systematic negative offset (mean =
`r sprintf("%.2f", comp_hvr$mean)`, SD =
`r sprintf("%.2f", comp_hvr$sd)`, 95% CI
[`r sprintf("%.2f", comp_hvr$ci_95["lower"])`,
`r sprintf("%.2f", comp_hvr$ci_95["upper"])`]),
while HC z-scores were closer to expected values
(mean = `r sprintf("%.2f", comp_hc$mean)`, SD =
`r sprintf("%.2f", comp_hc$sd)`;
@fig-normative-validation B). Age-bin-specific
calibration (@fig-normative-validation, panel B)
showed mean z-scores near zero in younger bins with
increasingly negative offset in older bins,
consistent with the age mismatch.

```{r fig-normative-validation}
#| fig-cap: "**Normative transfer validation.** (A) Age distributions: UK Biobank normative sample (yellow) vs ADNI comparable subsample (cyan); dashed lines indicate sample means. (B) Forest plot of HVR (purple) and HC (green) z-scores (mean ± 95% CI) by age bin; reference line at z = 0 indicates perfect calibration; open circles = age bins, filled circles = overall. (C) Z-score distributions for HVR (left) and HC (right) compared to the expected N(0,1) (yellow)."
#| fig-width: 6.5
#| fig-height: 6

stopifnot(!is.null(nv_data))
ukb_d <- nv_data$comparable_subsample$demographics$ukb_normative
adni_d <- nv_data$comparable_subsample$demographics$adni_comparable

p_age <- plot_age_distribution_comparison(
  ukb_age_mean = ukb_d$age_mean,
  ukb_age_sd = ukb_d$age_sd,
  ukb_age_min = ukb_d$age_min,
  ukb_age_max = ukb_d$age_max,
  ukb_n = ukb_d$n_total,
  adni_age_mean = adni_d$age_mean,
  adni_age_sd = adni_d$age_sd,
  adni_n = adni_d$n_total
)

p_forest <- plot_zscore_validation_forest(nv_data)

# Build z-score data.table with both measures
hvr_z.v <- comp_hvr$zscores
hc_z.v <- comp_hc$zscores
stopifnot(!is.null(hvr_z.v), !is.null(hc_z.v))
n_max <- max(length(hvr_z.v), length(hc_z.v))
zscore.dt <- data.table(
  PTID = seq_len(n_max),
  HVR_z = hvr_z.v[seq_len(n_max)],
  HC_z = hc_z.v[seq_len(n_max)]
)

p_dist <- plot_zscore_distribution(
  zscore.dt, sample_label = "Comparable"
)

(p_age | p_forest) / p_dist +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1)) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
```


:::{=latex}
\FloatBarrier
\newpage
\setcounter{table}{0}
\setcounter{figure}{0}
\renewcommand{\thetable}{B\arabic{table}}
\renewcommand{\thefigure}{B\arabic{figure}}
:::

# Methodological Validations

## CVR MIMIC Measurement Invariance

Measurement invariance of the CVR MIMIC model was
tested across sex in four steps (configural, metric,
scalar, strict; @tbl-mi).

```{r tbl-mi}
#| tbl-cap: "**CVR MIMIC measurement invariance.** Configural = same structure; Metric = equal loadings; Scalar = equal intercepts; Strict = equal residuals. ΔCFI < 0.01 and ΔRMSEA < 0.015 indicate acceptable invariance."

stopifnot(!is.null(cvr_mi.res))
require_output(
  create_mi_table(
    cvr_mi.res,
    "Measurement Invariance Across Sex"
  ),
  "tbl-mi"
)
```

## EDT Linearity and Simulation Validation

The linear time specification was validated via LME
likelihood ratio tests comparing linear vs quadratic
EDT across all sex-domain models (see
@tbl-linearity).

```{r tbl-linearity}
#| tbl-cap: "**EDT linearity validation.** LME models testing linear vs quadratic EDT specification. LRT tests whether adding a quadratic EDT term significantly improves fit. ΔAIC = AIC(linear) − AIC(quadratic); positive values favor quadratic. EDT = Estimated Disease Time."

require_output(
  create_linearity_table(
    edt_linearity,
    title = "EDT Linearity Validation"
  ),
  "tbl-linearity"
)
```

```{r edt-linearity-narrative}
#| include: false

stopifnot(!is.null(edt_linearity))
lt <- as.data.table(
  edt_linearity$results_table
)
n_sig <- sum(
  lt$LRT_p < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE
)
n_total <- nrow(lt)
if (n_sig == 0) {
  edt_lin_text <- paste0(
    "No domain showed a significant",
    " quadratic EDT term",
    " (all p > 0.05), supporting the",
    " linear time specification for",
    " the LGCM."
  )
} else {
  sig_doms <- lt[LRT_p < 0.05, Domain]
  max_chisq <- max(
    lt$LRT_chisq, na.rm = TRUE
  )
  edt_lin_text <- sprintf(
    paste0(
      "%s models showed significant",
      " quadratic EDT terms",
      " (%s; max χ² = %.1f).",
      " Large test statistics reflect",
      " substantial power afforded by",
      " sample size and repeated",
      " observations. A linear",
      " specification was adopted as a",
      " pragmatic compromise given",
      " convergence failure of quadratic",
      " parallel process LGCMs",
      " (see Methods)."
    ),
    sub("^(.)", "\\U\\1",
      n_of_total.fn(n_sig, n_total),
      perl = TRUE
    ),
    paste(
      DOMAIN_LABELS[sig_doms],
      collapse = ", "
    ),
    max_chisq
  )
}
```

`r edt_lin_text`

## SE-Constrained LGCM Validation

A Monte Carlo simulation
(`r simulation.res$config$n_reps` replications at
N = `r paste(simulation.res$config$sample_sizes, collapse = " and N = ")`)
compared SE-constrained LGCM (residual variance
fixed to SE²) against free estimation.

```{r simulation-interpretation}
#| include: false

stopifnot(!is.null(sim_conv))
fmt_sci <- function(x) {
  if (abs(x) < 1e-10) return("0")
  exp <- floor(log10(abs(x)))
  mant <- x / 10^exp
  sprintf("%.1f × 10^%d^", mant, exp)
}
sim_text_parts <- vapply(
  seq_len(nrow(sim_conv)), function(i) {
    n_reps <- simulation.res$config$n_reps
    sprintf(
      paste0(
        "At N = %d, the SE-constrained",
        " approach converged in %d of %d",
        " replications (free estimation:",
        " %d of %d) with bias = %s",
        " (free: %s), RMSE = %.3f",
        " (free: %.3f), and 95%% CI",
        " coverage = %.1f%%",
        " (free: %.1f%%)"
      ),
      sim_conv$n[i],
      sim_conv$n_converged_se[i], n_reps,
      sim_conv$n_converged_free[i], n_reps,
      fmt_sci(sim_conv$bias_se[i]),
      fmt_sci(sim_conv$bias_free[i]),
      sim_conv$rmse_se[i],
      sim_conv$rmse_free[i],
      sim_conv$coverage_se[i] * 100,
      sim_conv$coverage_free[i] * 100
    )
  }, character(1)
)
sim_text <- paste(
  sim_text_parts, collapse = ". "
)
```

`r sim_text`. Both approaches showed comparable
performance, validating the SE-constrained
specification used in the primary analyses.

## LME Covariate Effects

Full covariate effects from the sex-stratified
LME 3-way interaction models are presented for
the FRS model (@tbl-lme-covariates) and the
CVR~mimic~ model (@tbl-cvr-lme-covariates).

```{r tbl-lme-covariates}
#| tbl-cap: "**FRS LME covariate effects.** Sex-stratified. Bold indicates p < 0.05."

require_output(
  create_lme_covariate_table(
    frs_hvr, cvr_label = "FRS",
    title = "Covariate Effects (FRS Model)"
  ),
  "tbl-lme-covariates"
)
```

```{r tbl-cvr-lme-covariates}
#| tbl-cap: "**CVR~mimic~ LME covariate effects.** Sex-stratified. Bold indicates p < 0.05."

require_output(
  create_lme_covariate_table(
    cvr_hvr,
    cvr_label = "CVR~mimic~",
    title = md(
      "Covariate Effects (CVR~mimic~ Model)"
    )
  ),
  "tbl-cvr-lme-covariates"
)
```


:::{=latex}
\FloatBarrier
\newpage
\setcounter{table}{0}
\setcounter{figure}{0}
\renewcommand{\thetable}{C\arabic{table}}
\renewcommand{\thefigure}{C\arabic{figure}}
:::

# Sensitivity Analyses

All sensitivity checks converge on the same
pattern: FRS effects trace to age-weighting,
CVR~mimic~ effects are null or isolated, and the
HVR–cognition coupling (b-path) is consistent. Main
findings are stable across alternative brain
measures (HC volume), raw scores, attrition
adjustments, and EDT specification checks. Full
details follow.

## LME Sensitivity

### HC z-score (Total Hippocampal Volume)

HC z-score effects were generally weaker than HVR
z-score effects, with fewer significant 3-way
interactions, supporting the
hippocampus-to-ventricle ratio as a more sensitive
brain aging biomarker. The LME 3-way interaction was
repeated with HC z-score instead of HVR; results
appear in @tbl-hc-comparison, with covariate effects
in @tbl-hc-covariates and @tbl-hc-covariates-cvr.

```{r tbl-hc-comparison}
#| tbl-cap: "**HC z-score sensitivity analysis.** LME 3-way interaction effects (YRS × CVR × HC z-score) for total hippocampal volume. FRS and CVR~mimic~ compared side-by-side. Bold indicates p < 0.05."

require_output(
  create_lme_comparison_table(
    frs_hc, cvr_hc,
    summary_dt = hc_summary.dt
  ),
  "tbl-hc-comparison"
)
```

```{r tbl-hc-covariates}
#| tbl-cap: "**FRS HC z-score covariate effects.** Sex-stratified. Bold indicates p < 0.05."

require_output(
  create_lme_covariate_table(
    frs_hc, cvr_label = "FRS",
    title = "HC Covariate Effects (FRS Model)"
  ),
  "tbl-hc-covariates"
)
```

```{r tbl-hc-covariates-cvr}
#| tbl-cap: "**CVR~mimic~ HC z-score covariate effects.** Sex-stratified. Bold indicates p < 0.05."

require_output(
  create_lme_covariate_table(
    cvr_hc, cvr_label = "CVR~mimic~",
    title = md(
      "HC Covariate Effects (CVR~mimic~ Model)"
    )
  ),
  "tbl-hc-covariates-cvr"
)
```

```{r hc-interpretation}
#| include: false

stopifnot(!is.null(hc_summary.dt))
hc_p <- hc_summary.dt[
  Analysis == "Primary_Stratified"
]
frs_hc_sig <- hc_p[
  CVR_Measure == "FRS" & Significant == TRUE
]
cvr_hc_sig <- hc_p[
  CVR_Measure == "CVR_mimic" &
    Significant == TRUE
]
frs_hc_desc <- if (nrow(frs_hc_sig) > 0) {
  paste(
    sprintf(
      "%s %s",
      frs_hc_sig$Sex,
      DOMAIN_LABELS[frs_hc_sig$Domain]
    ),
    collapse = " and "
  )
} else "no sex–domain combination"
cvr_hc_desc <- if (nrow(cvr_hc_sig) > 0) {
  paste(
    sprintf(
      "%s %s",
      cvr_hc_sig$Sex,
      DOMAIN_LABELS[cvr_hc_sig$Domain]
    ),
    collapse = " and "
  )
} else "any sex–domain combination"
hc_interp_text <- sprintf(
  paste0(
    "The FRS × HC × YRS interaction was",
    " significant for %s, whereas",
    " CVR~mimic~ × HC × YRS %s"
  ),
  frs_hc_desc,
  if (nrow(cvr_hc_sig) > 0) {
    sprintf(
      "reached significance only for %s",
      cvr_hc_desc
    )
  } else {
    paste0(
      "did not reach significance for ",
      cvr_hc_desc
    )
  }
)
```

`r hc_interp_text`. HC z-score showed weaker effects than HVR z-score, supporting use of the ratio measure.

```{r hc-vs-hvr-synthesis}
#| include: false

hc_p2 <- hc_summary.dt[
  Analysis == "Primary_Stratified"
]
hvr_p <- lme_summary.dt[
  Analysis == "Primary_Stratified"
]
n_hc_sig <- sum(
  hc_p2$Significant, na.rm = TRUE
)
n_hvr_sig <- sum(
  hvr_p$Significant, na.rm = TRUE
)
hc_synth_text <- sprintf(
  paste0(
    "Significant 3-way interactions emerged",
    " in %s models for HC z-score versus",
    " %s for HVR z-score. The weaker HC",
    " effects support the specificity of",
    " HVR: capturing ventricular expansion",
    " alongside hippocampal atrophy",
    " provides greater sensitivity to",
    " CVR moderation."
  ),
  n_of_total.fn(n_hc_sig, 12L),
  n_of_total.fn(n_hvr_sig, 12L)
)
```

`r hc_synth_text`

### Attrition Analysis

Baseline characteristics were compared between
completers (≥4 visits) and dropouts (<4 visits);
results appear in @tbl-attrition-baseline.

```{r tbl-attrition-baseline}
#| tbl-cap: "**Attrition analysis.** Baseline characteristics of completers (≥4 visits) vs dropouts (<4 visits). Bold rows indicate p < 0.05. Completers = participants with sufficient visits for quadratic trend estimation."

require_output(
  create_attrition_table(attrition_analysis),
  "tbl-attrition-baseline"
)
```

```{r attrition-interpretation}
#| include: false

stopifnot(
  !is.null(attrition_analysis),
  !is.null(attrition_analysis$comparison_table)
)
comp_dt <- attrition_analysis$comparison_table
sig_vars <- comp_dt[p_value < 0.05, Variable]
var_display <- c(
  AGE = "age", EDUC = "education",
  FRS = "FRS", HVR_z = "HVR z-score",
  HC_z = "HC z-score", SEX = "sex",
  APOE4 = "APOE ε4 carrier status"
)
sig_labels <- vapply(
  sig_vars, function(v) {
    if (v %in% names(var_display)) {
      var_display[v]
    } else v
  }, character(1)
)
if (length(sig_vars) > 0) {
  attrition_text <- sprintf(
    paste0(
      "Significant baseline differences",
      " between completers and dropouts",
      " were observed in %s (all p < 0.05;",
      " see @tbl-attrition-baseline)."
    ),
    paste(sig_labels, collapse = ", ")
  )
} else {
  attrition_text <- paste0(
    "No significant baseline",
    " differences between completers",
    " and dropouts were observed",
    " (see @tbl-attrition-baseline),",
    " suggesting attrition was random",
    " with respect to measured variables."
  )
}
```

`r attrition_text`

```{r attrition-logistic-inline}
#| include: false

stopifnot(
  !is.null(attrition_analysis),
  !is.null(attrition_analysis$logistic)
)
log_model <- attrition_analysis$logistic
or_dt <- as.data.table(log_model$odds_ratios)
setnames(
  or_dt,
  c("2.5 %", "97.5 %"),
  c("CI_lower", "CI_upper"),
  skip_absent = TRUE
)
or_dt <- or_dt[term != "(Intercept)"]
or_dt[, sig := CI_lower > 1 | CI_upper < 1]

# Readable term labels
term_display <- c(
  FRS = "FRS", AGE = "age",
  APOE4TRUE = "APOE ε4 carrier status",
  SEXMale = "male sex",
  HVR_z = "HVR z-score",
  HC_z = "HC z-score"
)
or_dt[, term_label := fifelse(
  term %in% names(term_display),
  term_display[term], term
)]

sig_preds <- or_dt[sig == TRUE]
if (nrow(sig_preds) > 0) {
  items <- sprintf(
    "%s (OR = %.2f, 95%% CI [%.2f, %.2f])",
    sig_preds$term_label,
    sig_preds$OR,
    sig_preds$CI_lower,
    sig_preds$CI_upper
  )
  logistic_text <- sprintf(
    paste0(
      "Logistic regression predicting",
      " dropout identified %d significant",
      " predictor%s — %s."
    ),
    nrow(sig_preds),
    ifelse(nrow(sig_preds) > 1, "s", ""),
    paste(items, collapse = "; ")
  )
} else {
  logistic_text <- paste0(
    "Logistic regression predicting",
    " dropout identified no significant",
    " predictors (all 95% CIs included 1)."
  )
}

# FRS direction narrative
comp_dt <- attrition_analysis$comparison_table
frs_row <- comp_dt[
  grepl("FRS", Variable, ignore.case = TRUE)
]
frs_dir_text <- ""
if (nrow(frs_row) > 0) {
  c_mean <- frs_row$Completers_mean[1]
  d_mean <- frs_row$Dropouts_mean[1]
  if (!is.null(c_mean) && !is.null(d_mean) &&
      !is.na(c_mean) && !is.na(d_mean)) {
    direction <- if (d_mean > c_mean) {
      "higher"
    } else "lower"
    frs_dir_text <- sprintf(
      paste0(
        " Dropouts had %s baseline FRS",
        " (M = %.2f) than completers",
        " (M = %.2f), consistent with",
        " selective loss of higher-risk",
        " participants — this may",
        " attenuate observed CVR effects."
      ),
      direction, d_mean, c_mean
    )
  }
}
```

`r logistic_text``r frs_dir_text`

### Raw Brain Measure Sensitivity

The LME 3-way interaction was repeated using raw
(un-normalized) HVR and raw HC (with total
intracranial volume [TIV] covariate),
with FRS as predictor, full-sample, no sex
stratification, and no YRS² term.

```{r raw-hvr-inline}
#| include: false

stopifnot(
  !is.null(lme_raw_sens.res),
  !is.null(lme_raw_sens.res$results)
)
hvr_raw <- lme_raw_sens.res$results$HVR_raw_FRS
stopifnot(!is.null(hvr_raw))
raw_hvr_results <- list(results = hvr_raw)

hc_raw <- lme_raw_sens.res$results$HC_raw_FRS
stopifnot(!is.null(hc_raw))
raw_hc_results <- list(results = hc_raw)

fmt_raw_sig <- function(res) {
  sig.v <- c()
  nonsig.v <- c()
  for (d in c("MEM", "LAN", "EXF")) {
    r <- res$results[[d]]$interaction
    if (is.null(r)) next
    entry <- sprintf(
      "%s (β = %.4f, %s)",
      DOMAIN_LABELS[d], r$beta,
      fmt_p_inline(r$p_value)
    )
    if (r$p_value < 0.05) {
      sig.v <- c(sig.v, entry)
    } else {
      nonsig.v <- c(nonsig.v, entry)
    }
  }
  list(sig = sig.v, nonsig = nonsig.v)
}
hvr_raw_split <- fmt_raw_sig(raw_hvr_results)
hc_raw_split <- fmt_raw_sig(raw_hc_results)
raw_hvr_prose <- if (
  length(hvr_raw_split$sig) == 3
) {
  paste0(
    "Raw HVR was significant for all three",
    " domains: ",
    paste(hvr_raw_split$sig, collapse = ", ")
  )
} else if (length(hvr_raw_split$sig) > 0) {
  paste0(
    "Raw HVR was significant for ",
    paste(
      hvr_raw_split$sig, collapse = " and "
    ),
    if (length(hvr_raw_split$nonsig) > 0)
      paste0(
        ", but not ",
        paste(
          hvr_raw_split$nonsig,
          collapse = " or "
        )
      )
    else ""
  )
} else {
  "Raw HVR was not significant for any domain"
}
raw_hc_prose <- if (
  length(hc_raw_split$sig) > 0 &&
    length(hc_raw_split$nonsig) > 0
) {
  paste0(
    "Raw HC (+TIV) reached significance",
    " only for ",
    paste(
      hc_raw_split$sig, collapse = " and "
    )
  )
} else if (length(hc_raw_split$sig) == 0) {
  paste0(
    "Raw HC (+TIV) was not significant",
    " for any domain"
  )
} else {
  paste0(
    "Raw HC (+TIV) was significant for ",
    paste(hc_raw_split$sig, collapse = ", ")
  )
}
raw_meas_text <- paste0(
  raw_hvr_prose, ". ", raw_hc_prose
)

# Concordance check
sig_hvr <- sapply(
  names(raw_hvr_results$results),
  function(d) {
    r <- raw_hvr_results$results[[d]]$interaction
    !is.null(r) && r$p_value < 0.05
  }
)
if (any(sig_hvr)) {
  raw_concord_text <- sprintf(
    paste0(
      "Raw HVR × FRS was significant for",
      " %s, confirming normative z-scoring",
      " does not drive primary findings."
    ),
    paste(
      DOMAIN_LABELS[names(sig_hvr)[sig_hvr]],
      collapse = ", "
    )
  )
} else {
  raw_concord_text <- paste0(
    "Raw HVR × FRS was not significant",
    " for any domain, suggesting",
    " normative z-scoring enhances",
    " sensitivity."
  )
}

# Raw HC (+TIV) note
sig_hc <- sapply(
  names(raw_hc_results$results),
  function(d) {
    r <- raw_hc_results$results[[d]]$interaction
    !is.null(r) && r$p_value < 0.05
  }
)
raw_hc_note <- if (!any(sig_hc)) {
  paste0(
    " Raw HC (+TIV) was not significant",
    " for any domain, reinforcing the",
    " added sensitivity of the ratio",
    " measure."
  )
} else ""
```

`r raw_meas_text`. `r raw_concord_text``r raw_hc_note`

### EDT Moderation of LME Effects

A 4-way interaction (CVR × HVR × EDT × YRS) tested
whether CVR effects vary by disease stage.

```{r edt-moderation-inline}
#| include: false

build_edt_text <- function(edt_res, cvr_label) {
  stopifnot(
    !is.null(edt_res),
    !is.null(edt_res$summary_continuous)
  )
  sc <- as.data.table(edt_res$summary_continuous)
  parts <- vapply(
    c("Male", "Female"), function(sx) {
      sx_rows <- sc[Sex == sx]
      sig <- sx_rows[Significant == TRUE]
      if (nrow(sig) > 0) {
        items <- sprintf(
          "%s (β = %.4f, %s)",
          DOMAIN_LABELS[sig$Domain],
          sig$Beta,
          sapply(sig$p_value, fmt_p_inline)
        )
        sprintf(
          paste0(
            "%s × HVR × EDT (%s):",
            " significant for %s"
          ),
          cvr_label, sx,
          paste(items, collapse = "; ")
        )
      } else {
        min_p <- min(
          sx_rows$p_value, na.rm = TRUE
        )
        sprintf(
          paste0(
            "%s × HVR × EDT (%s):",
            " not significant (min %s)"
          ),
          cvr_label, sx,
          fmt_p_inline(min_p)
        )
      }
    }, character(1)
  )
  paste(parts, collapse = ". ")
}
edt_mod_text <- paste(
  build_edt_text(frs_edt, "FRS"),
  build_edt_text(cvr_edt, "CVR~mimic~"),
  sep = ". "
)
```

`r edt_mod_text`. Limited EDT moderation suggests CVR effects on HVR-cognition coupling are relatively stable across disease stages.

### EDT Stage Sensitivity

Analyses were restricted to early and middle EDT
tertiles (excluding late-stage) to confirm effect
stability.

```{r edt-sensitivity-inline}
#| include: false

stopifnot(!is.null(edt_sens.res))
rt <- as.data.table(edt_sens.res$results_table)

meas_labels <- c(
  HVR_z = "HVR z-score",
  HC_z = "HC z-score"
)
dom_labels <- c(
  MEM = "Memory", LAN = "Language",
  EXF = "Executive Function"
)

edt_sens_parts <- vapply(
  unique(rt$Measure), function(meas) {
    mrt <- rt[Measure == meas]
    m_lab <- if (
      meas %in% names(meas_labels)
    ) meas_labels[meas] else meas
    items <- c()
    n_concord <- 0L
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(mrt))) {
      row <- mrt[i]
      d_lab <- if (
        row$Domain %in% names(dom_labels)
      ) dom_labels[row$Domain] else row$Domain
      pct <- if (
        !is.null(row$Beta_change_pct) &&
        !is.na(row$Beta_change_pct)
      ) {
        sprintf(
          ", %.1f%% change",
          row$Beta_change_pct
        )
      } else ""
      if (!is.null(row$Consistent) &&
          isTRUE(row$Consistent)) {
        n_concord <- n_concord + 1L
      }
      items <- c(items, sprintf(
        "%s (β = %.4f, %s%s)",
        d_lab, row$Beta_early_middle,
        fmt_p_inline(row$P_early_middle),
        pct
      ))
    }
    concord_note <- if (n_concord > 0) {
      sprintf(
        " (%d/%d concordant with full-sample)",
        n_concord, nrow(mrt)
      )
    } else ""
    sprintf(
      "%s: %s%s", m_lab,
      paste(items, collapse = "; "),
      concord_note
    )
  }, character(1)
)
edt_sens_text <- paste(
  edt_sens_parts, collapse = ". "
)
```

`r edt_sens_text`.
